name: Center For NYC Neighborhoods
description_short: HUD-approved non-profit providing free homeowner counseling for mortgage assistance, foreclosure prevention, and affordable housing protection across New York State.
description_long:
The Center for NYC Neighborhoods was established in 2008 by New York City leadership and private foundations to address the foreclosure crisis during the Great Recession. Since then, it has evolved into a comprehensive homeowner advocacy organization serving middle- and working-class families across New York State. The organization operates as a HUD-approved counseling agency, licensed mortgage banker, and Certified Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI), giving it credentials to deliver both advice and financial solutions.

The Center offers multiple free and low-cost services including mortgage payment assistance counseling, foreclosure prevention and exit strategies, scam prevention education, rehabilitation and retrofit financing guidance, and estate planning resources. They also maintain a network of partner organizations across all five NYC boroughs and throughout New York State, enabling localized support through neighborhood housing services and legal aid partners. The organization serves as the Freddie Mac Borrower Help Center for New York State, expanding its reach to distressed borrowers.

What distinguishes the Center is its multi-pronged approach combining financial counseling with legal advocacy and community partnerships. Rather than offering loans or credit products, they connect homeowners to existing assistance programs and provide expert guidance on navigating complex housing issues. Their focus on affordable homeownership as an equality issue—not just a financial transaction—shapes their mission and partnerships, which include legal services organizations, community development corporations, and neighborhood housing services.

The primary limitation is that the Center provides counseling and guidance rather than direct financial assistance. Homeowners still must qualify for and secure funding through external programs. Additionally, while they serve New York State broadly, their deepest network and resources appear concentrated in New York City's five boroughs. Those seeking immediate cash or loan products will need to be referred elsewhere.
address: 60 Broad St 38th Floor Suite 3850, New York, NY 10004

company_info.headquarters: 60 Broad St 38th Floor Suite 3850, New York, NY 10004

pros: HUD-approved housing counselor network with official federal credibility
Licensed mortgage banker status in New York State allows direct financial advice
Certified CDFI designation indicates rigorous nonprofit oversight and compliance
Serves as official Freddie Mac Borrower Help Center for entire New York State
Extensive partner network across all five NYC boroughs plus statewide coverage
Free counseling services with no upfront fees or hidden costs
Specializes in foreclosure prevention with documented track record of thousands helped
cons: Does not provide direct loans or grants—only counseling and program referrals
Relies on homeowners qualifying for external assistance programs, which may have strict requirements
Primary service delivery concentrated in New York City despite statewide mission
Website lacks specific data on success rates, average outcomes, or client testimonials
Limited online self-service tools; most help requires phone contact or in-person meetings
best_for: New York homeowners at risk of missing mortgage payments seeking free counseling
Families facing foreclosure who need legal and financial navigation support
Middle- and working-class homeowners exploring rehabilitation loans or retrofits
Homeowners seeking protection from common housing scams and predatory lending
diagnosis: The Center for NYC Neighborhoods is best for New York homeowners facing mortgage, foreclosure, or housing stability challenges who need expert, free counseling from a credible HUD-approved organization. The main caveat is that they provide guidance and referrals, not direct loans or emergency cash—homeowners must still qualify for external assistance programs to receive financial relief.
services: HUD-approved mortgage counseling and payment assistance guidance
Scam prevention education and fraud protection
Home rehabilitation and retrofit financing program navigation
Coastal community flood insurance and resilience guidance
Freddie Mac Borrower Help Center services
Community partnerships connecting homeowners to legal aid and housing services
Research and policy advocacy on homeowner protection
Phone support via 311 or direct lines (646-786-0888, 1-855-HOME-456)
Email and press inquiries for policy collaboration
